title: Global crude imports recover in July despite China slump
section_path: Global crude imports recover in July despite China slump
section_path_text: Global crude imports recover in July despite China slump
summary:
Main
By Jay Maroo
Global crude/condensate imports recovered on a m-o-m basis in July, reinforcing the view that global crude supply and demand balances will tighten over the second half of this year, despite indications of weaker Chinese imports. Global crude/condensate imports on a seasonal basis (mbd)
Global seaborne imports rose m-o-m by almost 900kbd in July to reach 41.7mbd. However, the rise in July follows on from a 2023 low of 40.8mbd set in June.
